content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

August 31, 1977

Senator Alan Robbins
The State Senate
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Senator Robbins:

As I explained to you on the phone this morning, the original intention of inviting legislators, their staffs, and their families to the "Star Wars" presentation did not raise any disclosure obligations for any individual legislator since Twentieth Century Fox was only making a gift of a single ticket to each legislator. That situation was changed by your honest attempts to find a way to ration one of the most popular tickets in town. If the movie tickets were given to legislators for distribution at their discretion all such tickets would be gifts to the legislator.

Now that you have arranged for an additional showing of "Star Wars" it is our understanding from your new letter that the invitation is now re-extended to all legislators, staffs, and their families. As a result each legislator who receives a ticket only receives a gift of that single ticket.

I would just like to remind you that although the receipt of a single ticket does not create a reportable obligation on the part of any legislator, additional gifts to a legislator from Twentieth Century Fox would be accumulated with the movie ticket for determining whether the $25 threshold limit of Section 87207(1) is reached.

I hope this settles the problems. If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to call.

Sincerely,

Lee C. Rosenthal
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

LCR:jo

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE (916) 322-6061 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST (916) 322-[illegible]

RON SORO
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 445-3121

DISTRICT OFFICE ADDRESS:
6530 VAN NUYS BLVD.
VAN NUYS, CA 91401
(213) 989-5394

ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT:
RICK BELLENS

OFFICE MANAGER:
GLADYS KEITH

LEGISLATIVE SECRETARY:
ANNETTE PORIN

STAFF SECRETARY
JUDY VERHAAG

SENIOR INTERN:
BRUCE ROSENBLUM

STAFF INTERN:
JODI GOODMAN

COMMITTEE:
LOCAL GOVERNMENT
VIA CHAIRMAN
JUDICIARY
LAND AND
FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS
INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS
MOTION PICTURE DEVELOPMENT
COUNCIL, CHAIRMAN
JOINT COMMITTEE ON THE
STRUCTURE OF THE JUDICIARY

Senator
ALAN ROBBINS

Representing the
San Fernando Valley

August 31, 1977

Mr. Lee C. Rosenthal
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Dear Lee:

Thank you for spending time with me on the phone yesterday
afternoon and this morning.

Pursuant to our final discussion, I am enclosing a copy of
a letter to be sent today which will confirm our general invitation
to all staff and families and will advise them of an additional
Star Wars screening at 9 P.M.

I am writing to confirm our understanding that with this later
letter and with this change in procedure, the informal advice
contained in your letter of August 12th will still prevail.
If you could return a confirming note to me this afternoon
it will be appreciated and settle the nerves of my overworked
staff.

It is truly a pleasure to work with a state employee who is as
interested in doing a good professional job as you.

My best regards.

Sincerely,

ALAN ROBBINS

RICH ROLLINGS

JODI GOODMAN

COMMITTEES:
LOCAL GOVERNMENT
VICE CHAIRMAN
JUDICIARY
INSURANCE AND
FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS
INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS
MOTION PICTURE DEVELOPMENT
COUNCIL CHAIRMAN
JOINT COMMITTEE ON THE
STRUCTURE OF THE JUDICIARY

Senator
ALAN ROBBINS
Representing the
San Fernando Valley

STAR WARS

Due to popular demand, and through the courtesy
of 20th Century Fox, a second screening of STAR
WARS has been arranged.

Thursday, September 1, 9:00 p.m.
Crest Theatre, 1013 "K" Street Mall

As a result of our expanded capacity, the August 15
letter from 20th Century limiting each office to six
tickets is now withdrawn and this invitation is extended to all legislators, all legislative staff,
and to the families of both. This letter is intended
to serve as both an invitation and as a ticket of
admission to the 9:00 p.m. show.

We regret that due to space limits, however, no more
tickets can be issued for the earlier time.

May the force be with you!

[signature]

ALAN ROBBINS, Chairman
Motion Picture Development Council

P.S. Copies of this "admission letter" are available
in quantity from either of the legislative members
of the MPDC, Assemblyman Herschel Rosenthal or
Senator Alan Robbins.

STAR WARS
